methods.description: This method involves surveying the cave to obtain data on its morphology using traditional surveying techniques and laser scanning. The survey provides a detailed map of the cave's structure, which is essential for further geological and archaeological analysis.

steps.description: The cave survey was the starting point to obtain data on the morphology of the cave. The part of the cave open for visitors (about 700 m long) was surveyed at a scale of 1: 200, the rest of the cave at a scale of 1: 500.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct cave survey to map morphology

steps.description: A standard procedure was used to determine the relative position of survey points by measuring the distance, azimuth and inclination angle. The Leica hand laser distometer and Suunto clino-compas were used.
steps.searchable_summary: measure survey points using distometer and compass

steps.description: The starting point outside the cave was stabilized with a GNSS receiver.
steps.searchable_summary: stabilize starting point with GNSS receiver

steps.description: Additional data on the morphology of the investigated part of the cave were obtained by laser scanning.
steps.searchable_summary: perform laser scanning for additional morphology data

steps.description: Leica Cyclone 3DR software was used to visualize the obtained data and to create a characteristic profile.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize data using Leica Cyclone 3DR

methods.description: This method involves analyzing sediment samples to determine their grain-size distribution and sedimentological characteristics, which are crucial for understanding the depositional environment and geological history.

steps.description: Sedimentological field data and sampling were acquired within the archaeological test trench.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sedimentological field data and samples

steps.description: The grain-size of the sediment was determined on five bulk samples from each observed lithological unit using the areometric method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine grain-size using areometric method

steps.description: High-resolution grain-size was determined using the laser diffraction method.
steps.searchable_summary: perform high-resolution grain-size analysis using laser diffraction

steps.description: The samples were treated with a 4% solution of tetra-sodium diphosphate decahydrate and deionized water against coagulation and dispersed for at least 6 h on a shaker.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with solution and disperse

steps.description: Each measurement was repeated five times and an average value was used for the grain-size distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat measurements and calculate average grain-size

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the mineral composition of sediment samples using XRPD to determine the provenance and characteristics of the material.

steps.description: Samples were treated with 10% HCl to remove the carbonates, and washed with H2O in an ultrasonic bath.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with HCl and wash in ultrasonic bath

steps.description: The grains were separated using sodium polytungstate.
steps.searchable_summary: separate grains using sodium polytungstate

steps.description: Thin slides were prepared from the separated material, and the composition was determined by counting up to 300 grains per sample using a polarizing microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides and analyze composition with microscope

steps.description: The mineral composition of samples was determined by X-ray powder diffraction (XRPD).
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRPD analysis on samples

methods.description: This method involves analyzing organic matter in sediment samples to understand the palaeoenvironment through palynofacies and palynomorphs analysis.

steps.description: Samples were treated with 4% solution of tetra-sodium diphosphate decahydrate, cold HCl, and HF to remove carbonates and silica.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples to remove carbonates and silica

steps.description: Heavy liquid was used to separate the organic matter from the undissolved inorganic components.
steps.searchable_summary: separate organic matter using heavy liquid

steps.description: Microscopic analyses were performed using microscopes and photomicrographs were taken.
steps.searchable_summary: perform microscopic analysis and capture photomicrographs

steps.description: Palynofacies analyses were performed according to classifications, and results were plotted in diagrams.
steps.searchable_summary: perform palynofacies analysis and plot results

methods.description: This method involves analyzing faunal remains to understand the palaeoenvironment and taphonomic processes.

steps.description: The material was dry sieved on site using a mesh size of 6 × 6 mm, bagged and named.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve and label faunal remains

steps.description: In the laboratory, the faunal remains were washed and dried.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry faunal remains

steps.description: A detailed palaeontological analysis was conducted, comparing fossil remains with collections.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct palaeontological analysis and compare with collections

steps.description: A detailed taphonomic study was conducted, examining bone modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: perform taphonomic study on bone modifications

methods.description: This method involves using luminescence dating to establish a chronological framework for cave deposits.

steps.description: Samples were taken using stainless steel cylinders driven into the freshly cleaned sediment and sealed light tight after sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: collect luminescence samples with steel cylinders

steps.description: Sample preparation and measurements were conducted using standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and measure samples using standard methods

steps.description: A single grain, post infrared, infrared stimulated single aliquot regenerative dose protocol was applied for the determination of the equivalent dose.
steps.searchable_summary: apply single grain protocol for dose determination

steps.description: Dose recovery experiments were conducted for both luminescence signals.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct dose recovery experiments
